title: pseudolycopodiella caroliniana
intro: Pseudolycopodiella caroliniana, also known as Carolina forked fern or Carolina forked spike moss, is a species of lycopodiaceous plants that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Pseudolycopodiella caroliniana is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported antiseptic and antiviral properties.
